body_txt: Before a duel begins, one character announces themselves to their opponent without any prompting, because they want their target to know just who is about to wreck them . Almost always serves as a clue that It's Personal . Causes can be Genocide Backfire or You Killed My Father . If the other character has caused suffering but has not wronged the speaker personally, they may be stepping up For Great Justice . It can lead to But for Me, It Was Tuesday , but is more likely to be caused by it. This trope is also very common in Japanese anime, manga, and videogames regardless of the situation: It was part of the Samurai code of honor to tell your opponent your name before fighting them; not doing so was considered rude. May directly accompany a Heroic Second Wind . note Which, for those of you wondering, is the new name of the old trope that used to be called My Name Is Inigo Montoya. Compare Transformation Name Announcement , In the Name of the Moon , Say My Name , I Am X, Son of Y , Verbal Business Card , I Am Your Opponent . See also My Name Is ??? , Badass Boast . Contrast Full-Name Ultimatum , where someone else saying your name spells bad news for you, And This Is for... , where the hero dedicates a strike to someone that the villain has hurt.
